steps.title: Identify the key signal — entirely absent means zero observations
steps.content: The passage states “did not observe even one ______” and emphasizes that some species were “entirely absent from the tide pool at particular times of the year.” This requires a species-and-time combination where the population count was zero. The graph displays population data for each species across four months; I need to find which species shows a zero value at the specified time(s).

steps.title: Match the graph data to the choices
steps.content: Striated rockskipper appears on the graph with zero individuals in both January and April of 2001. This directly illustrates the example: a species entirely absent at particular times of the year. The other three options describe species—combtooth blenny, barred flagtail, and barred flagtail again—that all show nonzero populations during their stated months, contradicting the “did not observe even one” claim. This matches choice B — B is correct.
